title: Digital Divide Measurement And School Internet Connectivity In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping school-level internet connectivity and digital-divide gaps at sub-national scale in low-income countries, with explicit connectivity-classification methodology documentation, data-currency assessment, and the distinction between connectivity existence and verified digital-learning use.
why_it_matters: UNICEF and ITU estimate 1.3 billion school-age children lack home internet and two-thirds of schools in low-income countries have no internet connection. The COVID-19 pandemic exposed this divide — children without connectivity lost education entirely. Giga has mapped 1 million+ schools across 30 countries, but connectivity-classification methodology varies, data currency is uneven, and connectivity alone does not close the divide without electricity, devices, and teacher training. Without verified connectivity-gap analysis, infrastructure investment cannot be evidence-targeted.

success_metrics.target: Every connectivity claim documents the classification methodology used (satellite-derived, school census, mobile-network proxy) and its validation against ground-truth data.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against connectivity-classification documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every connectivity claim documents the data collection date and explicitly states whether data reflects current conditions or potentially outdated census records.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms data-currency assessment for each analyzed country.

success_metrics.target: No digital-learning claim is merged without documenting that connectivity existence does not equal verified educational use — electricity, devices, teacher digital literacy, and content are all necessary conditions.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms connectivity-vs-usage distinction in every effectiveness claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, connectivity-classification safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
